title: symphyotrichum falcatum subsp. commutatum
intro: Symphyotrichum falcatum subsp. commutatum, commonly known as prairie bent or white sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating a variety of health issues. The medicinal properties of Symphyotrichum falcatum subsp. commutatum can be attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, providing health benefits such as improved immune function and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
